pi_items.verified_value: GARCH(1,1) - real econometric model for modeling time-varying volatility in financial/macroeconomic time series
pi_items.source: Confirmed via web search: GARCH(1,1) is standard volatility model, implemented in Gretl with 'garch' command. Task description requests GARCH(1,1) model estimation.

pi_items.verified_value: Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition (Hill, Griffiths, Lim, 2018) dataset package from learneconometrics.com or POE5 official repository
pi_items.source: install_gretl.sh lines 123-175 document POE5 data download from https://www.learneconometrics.com/gretl/poe5/poe5data.zip with fallback to sourceforge. README.md confirms Hill, Griffiths, Lim authorship.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real macroeconomic time series data from the Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition (POE5) dataset package. The usa.gdt file contains 103 quarterly observations of US GDP and inflation rates from 1984 Q1 to 2009 Q3, sourced from the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis FRED database (series GDPC96 for real GDP and CPIAUCSL for CPI inflation rate). The target variable 'inf' represents the CPI inflation rate annualized as a percentage. The minimum observation requirement of 50 is easily satisfied by the 103 available observations. The task requires estimating a GARCH(1,1) volatility model using Gretl 2022a to model time-varying volatility in inflation rates.
